name: Misfiled Prophecy (Act II: The Endless Archives)
text: A prophecy about a chosen one has been filed under the wrong kingdom. The universe waits impatiently for a correction stamp.

choices.result_text: The prophecy updates itself with a sigh of cosmic resentment.
The prophecy is returned to sender. Somewhere, destiny loses a tracking number.

name: Haunted Suggestion Box (Act II: The Endless Archives)
text: A suggestion box rattles on the wall. The complaints inside appear to be written by people who have been dead for centuries.

choices.result_text: The dead are very specific, very angry, and surprisingly helpful.

choices.text: Seal the box with official tape.
choices.result_text: The box continues rattling, but now in a more compliant manner.

name: Unionized Broom Closet (Act II: The Endless Archives)
text: Several brooms have organized a union meeting inside a closet. Their demands include better bristles and fewer heroic montages.

choices.result_text: The brooms respect your paperwork and improve one of your procedures.
Nothing is solved, but everyone feels represented.

name: Lost-and-Found Desk (Act II: The Endless Archives)
text: Behind a dusty desk lies a pile of forgotten equipment, misplaced forms, and one suspiciously warm stapler.

choices.result_text: You find something that might become useful after several layers of approval.
Your deck feels slightly less cluttered, at least in spirit.

name: The Archive Supply Cart
text: A vendor unfolds a counter covered in seals, receipts, and suspiciously glowing merchandise.

choices.result_text: The prices are official, which somehow makes them worse.
You leave before a handling fee can be invented.

name: The Self-Correcting Index
text: An index the size of a cottage snaps open before you. Its entries rearrange themselves whenever you look away.

choices.result_text: The index makes several stern improvements. One of your cards returns with sharper margins.
The index screams in alphabetical order. Your deck feels lighter.

name: The Locked Reading Room
text: A locked reading room hums behind a brass door. The sign says silence is enforced by old magic and older librarians.

choices.result_text: You learn something powerful, although the silence leans on your lungs like a stack of ledgers.
The door remains locked. Somewhere inside, a chair approves of your manners.

name: The Borrower Who Never Returned
text: A translucent borrower wanders between the shelves, still looking for a book due several reigns ago.

choices.result_text: The search is exhausting, but the borrower remembers a trick from a very old appendix.
The notes are faded, useful, and faintly apologetic.

name: The Shelf That Shelves Back
text: A shelf blocks your path. It appears to be sorting travellers by usefulness.

choices.result_text: The shelf removes something it considers unsuitable. It refuses to explain its criteria.
You win the argument, but only because the shelf runs out of brackets.

name: The Margin Notes
text: A book lies open on a lectern. The margin notes are arguing with the printed text and winning.

choices.result_text: The notes teach you how to contradict reality with better formatting.
The book mutters that this will be cited later.

name: Unclaimed Reservation
text: A reservation slip bears your name, although you are fairly certain you never reserved a destiny.

choices.result_text: The volume opens at exactly the page you did not know you needed.
The slip vanishes. One unnecessary burden vanishes with it.

name: The Infinite Return Slot
text: A return slot yawns in the wall. It accepts books, forms, excuses, and possibly mistakes.

choices.result_text: The slot takes something from your deck and burps politely.
You pull out something useful. The slot keeps a small amount of you as a late fee.

name: Restocked Archive Supply Cart
text: A supply cart rattles into view, stacked with labels, keys, charms, and items that insist they are tax-deductible.

choices.result_text: The cart unfolds into a shop with entirely too many small drawers.
You leave before the cart can charge you for browsing.
